title: oxalis staphyleoides
intro: Oxalis staphyleoides, also known as the Violet Woodsorrel, is a perennial plant native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties due to the presence of oxalic acid. The health benefits of Oxalis staphyleoides include its ability to aid in digestion, reduce fever, and alleviate symptoms of colds and flu. Oxalis staphyleoides is commonly prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and can also be consumed raw or as a dried herb for its medicinal and health benefits.
